headlines.title: Canada and India Push for Major Free Trade Deal Focused on Energy and Tech
headlines.summary: Canada and India are negotiating a free trade deal anchored in energy, agri-food, and technology, Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney said, describing a potential agreement as a game changer for Canada’s businesses and workers. “We’re negotiating a free trade deal with India. headlines.title: Santos Doubles Down on Oil and LNG Expansion
headlines.summary: Australia’s Santos plans to prioritize growth in the production of crude oil and natural gas both at home and abroad, including in Alaska, the company said today, as quoted by Reuters. headlines.title: QatarEnergy Extends LNG Force Majeure Into August
headlines.summary: QatarEnergy said it would extend the force majeure on its exports of liquefied natural gas until the middle of August, Reuters has reported, citing Italy’s Edison, which has a long-term supply deal with the Qatari company. Originally, the force majeure was set to run until early

headlines.title: Australia Dodges LNG Disruption as Union Cancels Strike
headlines.summary: A strike threat at one of Australia’s biggest LNG facilities, Ichthys, has been averted after the trade union organizing the industrial action reported progress on negotiations with operator Inpex on pay and working conditions. headlines.title: Oil Prices Jump After U.S. Strikes Iranian Missile Sites
headlines.summary: Oil prices spiked in early Asian trade on Tuesday, after the U.S. military launched strikes on targets in southern Iran, reigniting fears of military action just a day after prices had dropped dramatically on promises of a peace deal. headlines.title: Oil Could Stay Above $100 for Years, Analysts Warn
headlines.summary: Oil prices opened trade this week with a decline on reports that a deal between the U.S. and Iran was imminent. Brent crude slipped below $100 for the first time in days. But then President Trump said there was no rush on a deal and the U.S blockade in Hormuz would remain. While

headlines.title: India Cuts Fuel Demand Growth Projections By 40% Amid Austerity DriveIndia Cuts
headlines.summary: India’s refined petroleum demand growth forecast has received a major downgrade, with rising crude oil import costs, a weaker rupee and government-led austerity and conservation measures expected to dramatically cut oil demand. According to Kpler, India’s refined products demand

headlines.title: Mexico’s Pemex Still Struggles Despite the Oil Price Surge
headlines.summary: Mexican state oil giant Pemex was spared a rating downgrade at Moody’s despite the agency slashing Mexico’s sovereign rating to just one notch above junk status. headlines.title: Asia's Energy Crisis Is Pushing Singapore Toward Nuclear
headlines.summary: Next year, the United Nations will assess whether Singapore is ready to produce nuclear energy.
